sprint.name: Sanders Intelligence — In-progress bucket (2026-05-15)
sprint.source: Asana project 'Sanders intelligence app', section 'In progress'
stack_reminders: TanStack Query v5 for data fetching; query keys ['inventory','latest'], ['inventory','trends'], etc.
Supabase Postgres + RLS; queries must paginate past 1000-row cap using .range() loop
Recharts for charts; existing color palette: success #4caf87, accent #6c8aff, danger #e05c7a, info #8890b5
lucide-react for icons; do not pull new icon libraries
Mutations must use try/catch/finally so loading state always clears
Avoid in-place .sort() on React Query cached arrays — always spread first ([...arr].sort())

shared_conventions.filter_dropdown_style: Use existing `select` Tailwind class; first option is 'All <thing>'; value '' means no filter; small uppercase label above (text-[10px] font-semibold text-text2 uppercase tracking-wider).
shared_conventions.sortable_header_style: Reuse the SortableTh pattern from ActionCenter.tsx (ChevronsUpDown when inactive, ArrowUp/ArrowDown when active, text-accent on active).
shared_conventions.currency_and_number_formatting: Use fmtCurrency / fmtNumber from @/lib/utils everywhere; never raw toFixed on $.
shared_conventions.url_param_filter_pattern: When linking to /purchasing/inventory pre-filtered, use encodeURIComponent and slash-encode 's/o' as 's%2Fo'.

tasks.title: Filter + sort SKUs in Task creation and Vendor View
tasks.summary: Two SKU tables that currently only support free-text search need explicit Vendor / Status / Category filter dropdowns plus column sorting. Out of scope: InventoryBrowser (which already has vendor+status filters and sortable headers).

surfaces.name: TaskModal SKU selector modal
surfaces.current_state: Inside the 'Select SKUs' Modal there is one search input that filters product_code | description | supplier_description | brand_name | category_name (see filterSkuSelectorRows). Columns SKU, Description, Vendor, Category, Status, On Hand, Days OH, Rec. Order, Order Value are static — no sort headers, no per-column filters.

surfaces.name: Vendor View expanded SKU list
surfaces.current_state: When a vendor row is expanded, child rows render via getVendorSkuRows(...) and have a separate sort state (expandedSkuSort) plus an expandedSkuStatus filter and expandedSkuSearch. Vendor (always = parent) and Category are not yet exposed as filter dropdowns on the expanded sub-table.

ui: Above the SKU table inside the selector modal, render a single horizontal filter bar with: keep existing search input on the left; add three select dropdowns to the right — Vendor, Status, Category — each with an 'All …' default.
Vendor options = distinct supplier_description from selectableSkus (sorted, non-empty).
Status options = the 6 raw inventory statuses in this order: Ok, Excess stock, Surplus orders, Potential s/o, Stocked out, New item.
Category options = distinct category_name from selectableSkus (sorted, non-empty).

Each column header in the SKU table becomes click-sortable: SKU, Description, Vendor, Category, Status, On Hand, Days OH, Rec. Order, Order Value. Use the SortableTh component pattern from ActionCenter.tsx. Default sort: 'Order Value' descending (preserves today's de-facto ordering).
When the user selects/unselects a row, do NOT change the sort order on the next render (today the sort puts selected rows on top — keep that as a SECONDARY sort, so primary sort still wins).
Active filter pills: if any of Vendor/Status/Category are set, show a 'Clear filters' link to reset them all.
logic: Extend filterSkuSelectorRows in app/src/components/tasks/TaskModal.helpers.ts (or add a sibling helper) with optional { vendor, status, category } args. Existing free-text search keeps current behavior.
Apply filters before sort. Apply sort before the existing 'selected first' tiebreaker. Apply the slice(0, 200) cap LAST.

Sort comparator: numeric fields (on_hand, days_on_hand, recommended_order, recommended_order_value) numeric ascending/descending; string fields use localeCompare. Status sort should follow severity order: Stocked out > Potential s/o > Excess stock > Surplus orders > New item > Ok (ascending = least → most severe).

ui: In the expanded sub-row block, alongside the existing search and Status dropdown, add a Category dropdown (Vendor is implied by the parent row so omit it).
Category options = distinct category_name from the parent vendor's records.

Promote the existing per-column sort to an explicit SortableTh row for: SKU, Description, Category, Status, On Hand, Days OH, Rec. Order Qty, Rec. Order Value. Reuse VendorSkuSortState and getVendorSkuRows; extend them if a new sort field is required.
Default expanded-list sort: Rec. Order Value desc.
logic: Extend getVendorSkuRows to accept an optional category filter and pass through.
Reset expandedSkuStatus, the new expandedSkuCategory, expandedSkuSearch, and expandedSkuSort whenever expandedVendor changes.

acceptance_criteria: From New Task → Vendor Order → Edit SKUs, a user can narrow the SKU pool by Vendor='Acme', Status='Stocked out', Category='Hardware' simultaneously and then sort by Order Value descending; selecting a row does not change the visible order beyond moving it to the secondary 'selected' group.
Clearing all three dropdowns plus the search restores the full pool (up to the 200-row cap).
On Vendor View, expanding a vendor and choosing Category='Hardware' shows only that category's SKUs for that vendor; sort headers toggle asc/desc and the active header shows the arrow + accent color.
Switching expansion to a different vendor resets filters and sort to defaults.
No regressions in InventoryBrowser (this task does not touch it).
All filter dropdowns and sort headers are keyboard accessible (native <select> / <th> + onClick — no custom-popover work needed).
out_of_scope: Saving filter preferences across sessions / users
Adding new columns to either table

tasks.title: Executive Summary: weekly stacked bar of inventory health ($ %)
tasks.summary: Add a new stacked bar chart to Executive Summary that shows inventory health distribution by $ value over time, bucketed by ISO week, expressed as percent of total per week.

data_pipeline.current_hook: useInventoryTrends() in app/src/hooks/useInventory.ts — fetches last 20 complete uploads and returns UploadTrendPoint[]; per-upload aggregates do NOT currently include per-status $ values, only totalValue/atRiskCount/excessValue/fillRate/totalRecOrderValue/totalSkus.
required_extension: Add fields to each upload aggregate (and to UploadTrendPoint) for the 4 health buckets, in $: okValue, excessValue (rename to keep semantic 'Excess stock + Surplus orders'), atRiskValue, newItemValue. Keep the existing fields for backward compat (excessValue is already used elsewhere; if its semantics are unchanged we can simply add okValue / atRiskValue / newItemValue).

Update the SELECT in fetchInventoryTrends to include on_hand_value AND status so per-status sums can be computed per upload. The existing query selects only on_hand_value/excess_value/recommended_order_value/average_sales/status — confirm status is already selected (it is, line 139). Add a per-status sum reducer.

weekly_bucketing.rule: ISO week (Mon–Sun). Compute week key as 'YYYY-Www' (e.g. 2026-W19) from upload date.
weekly_bucketing.aggregation_within_week: If a week contains multiple uploads, AVERAGE the four bucket values (okValue, excessValue, atRiskValue, newItemValue) for that week. Average AFTER computing each upload's totals, not by re-pooling raw rows.
weekly_bucketing.empty_weeks: Render as a visual gap on the X-axis (do not interpolate). Use a fixed week range = last N weeks where N is configurable in code (default N=12).
weekly_bucketing.x_axis_label: Short week label, e.g. 'May 11' = Monday of that ISO week.

data_pipeline.percent_of_total: For each weekly bucket, compute the four percentages = bucketValue / sum(four buckets) * 100. The stacked bar is the 4 percentages, summing to 100%.

ui.placement: Inside the Executive Summary 'Historical Trends — across uploads' grid (md:grid-cols-2). Add as a NEW card titled 'Inventory Health by Value — Weekly (%)' positioned as the first card in the trends section (above 'Total Inventory Value Over Time').

config.Tooltip: Show all four buckets with $ value (raw) AND %; e.g. 'OK — $124,500 (62%)'.

ui.drill_through: Clicking a segment navigates to /purchasing/inventory?status=<encoded raw status> using a representative raw status per bucket: OK→'Ok', Excess→'Excess stock', At Risk→'Potential s/o', New Items→'New item'. Only the latest-week bar segments need to be clickable; older weeks need not be interactive.

files_to_modify: app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx — add the new card and the per-week aggregator
Optional: app/src/lib/utils.ts — add a getIsoWeek(date) helper if not present
edge_cases: Fewer than 2 weeks of data → show the same 'Not enough data yet' card already used for trends, but specifically gated on weekly buckets count < 2.
A week with zero $ value across all 4 buckets (shouldn't happen in practice) → render an empty/zero bar (no NaN%).
Uploads with status values not in our 6-value enum → ignore for bucketing but still include in totalSkus.
acceptance_criteria: Card renders only when there are ≥2 distinct ISO-weeks of complete uploads available.
Each weekly bar sums to 100% (visually full height).
Bar segment colors and order: OK (green) bottom → Excess (blue) → At Risk (red) → New Items (gray) top.
Tooltip on hover shows raw $ AND % for each segment of that week.
Clicking a segment of the most recent week opens /purchasing/inventory pre-filtered by the corresponding status.
Multiple uploads in one ISO week produce a single bar whose values are the AVERAGE of those uploads.
No regressions to the existing four trend cards.
out_of_scope: Persisting weekly snapshots to a new table (we compute on the fly from uploads)
Splitting Excess into 'Excess stock' vs 'Surplus orders' — they remain merged in the Excess bucket

tasks.title: Collapse/Expand toggle for the navigation sidebar
tasks.summary: Add a button that toggles the sidebar between full-width (current 224 px / w-56) and a narrow icon-only rail (~56 px / w-14). State persists across reloads.
files_to_modify: app/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx (only if state needs to be hoisted; otherwise keep local)
changes: Add a `collapsed` boolean state, persisted to localStorage under key 'ui.sidebar.collapsed' (default false).

When collapsed: aside className width becomes w-14; group labels ('Purchasing', 'C-Suite', 'Work', 'Admin') are hidden; NavLink rows show icon only, centered, padding adjusts (px-0 py-2, justify-center); the logo area shrinks to just an 'S' monogram; the user footer hides name/role text but keeps the sign-out icon button with title='Sign out'.
When expanded: behaves exactly as today.

Toggle button: an icon button (PanelLeftClose / PanelLeftOpen from lucide-react) placed in the sidebar header (top-right of the logo area) OR sticky at the bottom of the nav scroll area — pick the header placement for visibility.
All NavLink rows in collapsed mode must have a native title attribute equal to the label so the user can hover to see the name.
Smooth width transition: Tailwind `transition-[width] duration-150 ease-out` on the aside.
When collapsed, the 'Sanders / Intelligence' two-line brand collapses to a single 'S' glyph (or short logo).
acceptance_criteria: Clicking the toggle shrinks the sidebar to ~56 px and back to 224 px with a smooth transition.
After reload the sidebar restores to the user's last state via localStorage.
In collapsed mode, hovering an icon shows the page name as a tooltip.
All routes remain navigable from the collapsed sidebar.
No layout shift / horizontal scrollbar appears on the main content during transition.
Sign-out button still works in collapsed mode (icon-only with title).
Works at md and lg breakpoints (mobile is not in scope of this task).
out_of_scope: Auto-collapse on narrow viewports / responsive mobile drawer

tasks.title: BUG: Sorting in one Action Center table affects other tables
tasks.summary: On /purchasing/action-center, clicking a sort header in one section (At Risk / Backorders / Overstock) appears to change the visible order of rows in other sections. Each section has its own SortState (arSort/boSort/osSort) so the contamination is happening upstream of the section sort.
files_to_investigate: app/src/hooks/useInventory.ts — analyzeInventory returns references into the same records[] array; if any consumer .sort()s atRiskItems/backorderItems/excessItems in place, the React Query cache is mutated and all observers see new ordering
diagnostic_steps: Add a top-of-component invariant check during dev: store a snapshot of inventory.records[0..4].product_code and assert it remains unchanged after a re-render triggered by clicking a sort header. If it changes, the cached array is being mutated.

Grep the entire src/ for `\.sort(` calls on arrays that come from useInventory/useInventoryAnalysis/useAtRiskItems/useBackorderItems/useExcessItems/useInboundItems. Any such call must be on a spread copy ([...arr].sort(...)) — flag and fix any in-place sort.
Specifically audit groupRecordsByVendorCategory — it builds Maps and arrays, so it does not mutate, but verify with a unit-style check.
Verify each section's sort state is fully isolated by clicking through sort headers in one section and screenshotting the other two before/after.
expected_fix_shape: Most likely fix: replace any `xxx.sort(...)` on a derived array with `[...xxx].sort(...)`. Or wrap the analyzeInventory output arrays in frozen wrappers in dev mode to surface mutations.

If the bug turns out to be perceived rather than actual cross-state coupling (e.g. the user is confused by the vendor/category grouping reshuffling rows within a sort), document the actual behavior and add a small caption under each section header explaining 'Rows grouped by vendor → category, then sorted within group by <field>'.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a sort header in the At Risk section does not change the row order of the Backorders or Overstock tables.
Clicking a sort header in Backorders does not change the row order of At Risk or Overstock.
Clicking a sort header in Overstock does not change the row order of At Risk or Backorders.

Navigating away and back to /purchasing/action-center preserves the same default sort per section (At Risk: days_on_hand asc; Backorders: unsatisfied_customer_orders_value desc; Overstock: excess_value desc).
Refreshing the data via React Query invalidation does not re-trigger the bug.
A regression test or in-component invariant (commented out for prod) is left behind so this doesn't silently come back.

tasks.title: Executive Summary 'Top Risk Supplier' list — replace columns
tasks.summary: Replace the column set on the 'Top Risk Supplier — Requires Attention' card in Executive Summary with $ -denominated metrics that are more actionable for executives.
files_to_modify: app/src/pages/csuite/ExecutiveSummary.tsx — `topRiskSuppliers` useMemo and the JSX list rendering

new_columns.label: # at-risk SKUs
new_columns.compute: count of records with status ∈ {Potential s/o, Stocked out}

new_columns.label: $ at risk
new_columns.compute: sum(on_hand_value) over the same at-risk subset for this vendor

new_columns.label: Lowest Days OH
new_columns.compute: Math.min(days_on_hand) across at-risk SKUs for this vendor

new_columns.label: Rec Order $
new_columns.compute: sum(recommended_order_value) across at-risk SKUs for this vendor

new_columns.label: Open Backorders $
new_columns.compute: sum(unsatisfied_customer_orders_value) across ALL of this vendor's records (not just at-risk subset)

new_columns.label: Fill rate
new_columns.compute: okCount / activeSkus * 100 where activeSkus = records with average_sales > 0 OR on_hand > 0, across vendor

columns_removed: Rec. Order quantity (replaced by Rec Order $)
logic_changes: topRiskSuppliers must now group ALL records by supplier, not only the at-risk subset, so that fillRate and openBackorderValue can be computed across the vendor's whole portfolio. Then derive the at-risk subset within each group for the at-risk-specific metrics.
Sort order: descending by Rec Order $ (preserve current default).
Vendor remains the row's primary label; sub-line under the vendor name shows '<atRiskSkuCount> at-risk SKUs · <fillRate.toFixed(1)>% fill rate' for at-a-glance scanning.

ui.row_layout: Keep the existing card-row pattern (border-l-2 border-danger, hover:bg-surface2/50). Replace the 5 right-side metric columns with the 6 new ones in this order: # at-risk SKUs, $ at risk, Lowest Days OH, Rec Order $, Open Backorders $, Fill rate. Use small text-xs label above value (text2) and font-semibold tabular-nums for the value. Lowest Days OH value is colored red (text-danger) when ≤ 7, warning when ≤ 14, otherwise default.
ui.click_through: Same as today — clicking the row navigates to /purchasing/inventory?status=Potential+s%2Fo&vendor=<encoded vendor>

acceptance_criteria: Card renders with exactly the 6 metrics listed above for each of the top 10 risk suppliers.
Default sort is by Rec Order $ descending; same order as before for vendors where Rec Order $ has not changed.
Fill rate matches what the same vendor would show in Vendor View (cross-reference for sanity).
$ at risk equals the sum of on_hand_value for the vendor's at-risk SKUs only — not their entire portfolio.
Open Backorders $ is the vendor's TOTAL across all their SKUs (not gated to at-risk).
If no at-risk suppliers exist, the existing 'inventory health is good' empty state is preserved.
Click-through still pre-filters Inventory Browser correctly.
No regressions to other parts of Executive Summary (KPI strip, $ health bar, pie/bar charts, historical trends).
out_of_scope: Adding column sorting controls on this list (it is rank-ordered, not user-sortable)
Showing categories per vendor as a separate column

global_acceptance: App builds with `cd app && npm run dev` and `npm run build` with no new TypeScript errors and no new console errors on first paint of each affected route.
No new ESLint warnings introduced in the files touched.
RLS-protected queries continue to paginate properly (no implicit 1000-row cap regressions).
All 5 in-progress Asana tasks can be moved to Done after manual smoke-test of the acceptance criteria on each task.
Each task is delivered as one focused commit (or one PR with logically-separated commits) so they can be reviewed independently.
The dismissed_actions migration (supabase/migrations/002_dismissed_actions.sql) is unrelated and not required by this sprint.
verification_plan: For Task 1: open New Task → Vendor Order → Edit SKUs; verify the three dropdowns and sort headers work; then expand a vendor on Vendor View and verify the same.

For Task 2: confirm the weekly bar appears in Executive Summary 'Historical Trends'; hover several bars and verify $ + % tooltip; click the most recent OK segment and confirm Inventory Browser opens filtered by 'Ok'.
For Task 3: toggle the sidebar, reload the page (state persists), navigate between pages (state persists), confirm tooltips on hover in collapsed mode.
For Task 4: open Action Center, screenshot all three tables, click sort headers in section A and B and C in sequence, confirm the OTHER tables in each step retain their row order.
For Task 5: open Executive Summary, confirm the 'Top Risk Supplier' card shows the 6 new metrics in the specified order, click a row and confirm Inventory Browser opens with the vendor pre-filtered.
